data.name: Server_AccessDenied_Format
data.value: Access denied for port {0} (run as admin)

data.name: Status_DownloadingInstallingMidi
data.value: Downloading and installing Windows MIDI Services…

data.name: Status_SDL3NotFoundDetail
data.value: SDL3.dll not found! Place SDL3.dll next to PadForge.exe. Download from https://github.com/libsdl-org/SDL/releases

data.name: Status_ViGEmNotDetected
data.value: ViGEmBus driver not detected. Virtual controller output disabled.

data.name: Status_InvalidClipboard
data.value: Clipboard does not contain valid PadForge settings.

data.name: Status_NoDeviceToCopyFrom
data.value: No device selected to copy from.

data.name: Status_NoOtherDevices
data.value: No other configured devices found to copy from.

data.name: Status_NoSettingsFile
data.value: No settings file found on disk.

data.name: Status_NoDeviceToRecord
data.value: No device connected to record from.

data.name: Status_RecordingPrompt_Format
data.value: Recording: press a button or move an axis for "{0}"...

data.name: Status_DeviceHidden
data.value: Device hidden. (Can be restored in settings file.)

data.name: Dashboard_DsuDescription
data.value: DSU/cemuhook server for gyro and accelerometer data.

data.name: Dashboard_DsuFooter
data.value: Provides gyro/accel data to Cemu, Dolphin, Yuzu, etc.

data.name: Dashboard_WebDescription
data.value: Serve a gamepad UI to browsers on your local network.

data.name: Dashboard_WebFooter
data.value: Connect from a phone or tablet browser to use as a gamepad.

data.name: Settings_InputEngineDesc
data.value: Configure polling behavior and background processing.

data.name: Settings_BackgroundPolling
data.value: Continue polling when window loses focus

data.name: Settings_HideDevicesTooltip
data.value: Master switch for HidHide device hiding and low-level input hooks. When off, no devices are hidden regardless of per-device settings.

data.name: Settings_HidHideDesc
data.value: Hide physical devices from games to prevent double input.

data.name: Settings_WhitelistDesc
data.value: These applications can still see hidden devices. PadForge is always whitelisted automatically.

data.name: Settings_ViGEmDesc
data.value: Virtual controller driver for Xbox 360 and DualShock 4 output.

data.name: Settings_VJoyDesc
data.value: Virtual joystick driver for custom DirectInput device output.

data.name: Settings_MidiDesc
data.value: SDK runtime for virtual MIDI device output. Downloaded from GitHub (~210 MB).

data.name: Settings_SettingsFileDesc
data.value: Save, reload, or reset your configuration.

data.name: Settings_LanguageDesc
data.value: Choose your preferred display language. Requires restart.

data.name: Settings_RestartRequired
data.value: Language change requires a restart to take effect.

data.name: About_Tagline
data.value: Modern fork of x360ce — rebuilt with SDL3, WPF, and .NET 10

data.name: About_Description
data.value: PadForge turns any input device into any virtual controller. Gamepads, joysticks, keyboards, mice, and touchscreens are mapped to Xbox 360, DualShock 4, DirectInput, Keyboard+Mouse, or MIDI output that games treat as real hardware — powered by ViGEmBus, vJoy, and Windows MIDI Services. It uses SDL3 for controller input, Windows Raw Input for keyboard and mouse, and provides a modern Fluent Design interface for mapping, dead zones, sensitivity curves, and force feedback.

data.name: About_VJoyDesc
data.value: Virtual joystick driver for custom DirectInput output

data.name: About_MidiServicesDesc
data.value: Virtual MIDI device output (Windows MIDI Services)

data.name: About_LicenseText
data.value: This software is licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International). 3D controller models adapted from Handheld Companion (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0). 2D controller assets from Gamepad-Asset-Pack by AL2009man (MIT). Original codebase forked from x360ce (MIT). SDL3 is licensed under the zlib License. Windows MIDI Services is licensed under the MIT License. ViGEmBus, vJoy, HelixToolkit, and WPF UI are licensed under the MIT License.

data.name: Devices_RemoveTooltip
data.value: Remove this device and its settings

data.name: Devices_ForceRaw
data.value: Force raw joystick mode (bypass gamepad remapping)

data.name: Devices_ForceRawTooltip
data.value: Read raw HID indices instead of SDL's gamepad mapping — use when SDL misidentifies button/axis layout

data.name: Devices_HideTooltip
data.value: Completely hides this device from other applications via the HidHide driver

data.name: Devices_HideTooltipInstall
data.value: Install the HidHide driver to enable this option

data.name: Devices_ConsumeTooltip
data.value: Suppresses only the specific keys/buttons that are mapped, letting unmapped inputs pass through

data.name: Devices_HideWarning_Format
data.value: You are about to {0}. This will affect {1}{2}.

data.name: Devices_HideAction
data.value: hide this device from all applications

data.name: Devices_ConsumeAction
data.value: block mapped inputs from this device

data.name: Devices_WarnHide_Format
data.value: If this is your only {0}, you will lose the ability to interact with your system. Only proceed if you have another input device available.

data.name: Devices_WarnConsumeMouse
data.value: If you map inputs that are critical for system interaction (e.g. left/right click, X/Y movement), you may lose the ability to control your system. Only proceed if you have another input device available.

data.name: Devices_WarnConsumeKeyboard
data.value: If you map inputs that are critical for system interaction (e.g. common keys), you may lose the ability to control your system. Only proceed if you have another input device available.

data.name: Pad_MidiCCCountTooltip
data.value: Number of CC outputs (max depends on Start CC)

data.name: Pad_MidiStartCCTooltip
data.value: First CC number (0-127). Higher values reduce max CC Count.

data.name: Pad_MidiNoteCountTooltip
data.value: Number of note outputs (max depends on Start Note)

data.name: Pad_MidiStartNoteTooltip
data.value: First MIDI note number (0-127, 60 = Middle C). Higher values reduce max Note Count.

data.name: Pad_MidiVelocityTooltip
data.value: Note-on velocity (0-127). How hard virtual keys are pressed.

data.name: Pad_MapAllTooltip
data.value: Sequentially record all mappings — press a button/axis for each prompt

data.name: Pad_MapAllOneByOne
data.value: Sequentially record all mappings one by one

data.name: Pad_MappingHint
data.value: Click a mapping row and press a button/axis on your controller to assign it.

data.name: Pad_CopyTooltip
data.value: Copy all mappings and settings to clipboard

data.name: Pad_PasteTooltip
data.value: Paste mappings and settings from clipboard

data.name: Pad_CopyFromTooltip
data.value: Copy settings from another configured device

data.name: Macro_AlwaysDesc
data.value: Runs continuously while this virtual controller is active. No trigger button required.

data.name: Macro_KeyComboHint
data.value: Select a key to add it. Example: {Control}{Alt}{Delete}

data.name: Macro_ProcessTooltip
data.value: Process name without .exe — dropdown shows processes with active audio sessions

data.name: Profiles_Description
data.value: Create per-application controller profiles that auto-switch based on the active game.

data.name: Profiles_AutoSwitch
data.value: Auto-switch profiles based on foreground application

data.name: CopyFrom_SelectDevice
data.value: Select a device to copy its settings from:

data.name: Pad_SensitivityCurveHint
data.value: Double-click chart to add point. Right-click to remove. Drag to adjust.

data.name: Pad_SensitivityCurveHintShort
data.value: Double-click to add point. Right-click to remove. Drag to adjust.

data.name: Macro_RecordHint
data.value: Press buttons or move an axis/trigger to record. Axes are detected when deflected past 50%.

data.name: Macro_SysVolumeHint
data.value: Continuously maps the selected axis (0–100%) to the Windows system volume, scaled to the volume limit. Use with "Always" trigger, or a button/axis trigger with "While Held" + "Until Release".

data.name: Macro_AppVolumeHint
data.value: Controls the volume of a specific application in the Windows volume mixer, scaled to the volume limit. Enter the process name (visible in Task Manager). Use with "Always" trigger, or a button/axis trigger with "While Held" + "Until Release".

data.name: Macro_MouseMoveHint
data.value: Continuously maps the selected axis to mouse movement (or scroll). Use with "Always" trigger, or a button/axis trigger with "While Held" + "Until Release". For stick-to-mouse, create two actions: one for X axis, one for Y axis.

data.name: Settings_AudioRumbleDesc
data.value: Drive controller rumble from system audio bass frequencies.
